spans.text: the sash with sullen sound , And freezing palsy creeps over all my frame
the sash with sullen sound , And freezing palsy
I in Knoyle's green shades thy steps attend
the high pines murmur thus above
I hear its Sabbath chime Come smoothly softened down the woody vale
its Sabbath chime Come smoothly softened down the woody vale
yonder lone eminence sublime , Fast whirling in the wind
that my once vacant mind Amid those scenes shall never more rejoice
Where in this hard world I have happiest been
this hard world I have happiest been
The kind companion of my devious way
our destined road far onward lay
my drooping head , Who dries with her dark locks the tender tear
intrude To mar the melancholy mind's repose
